title: Thai Fish Curry
description: A flavorful and aromatic Thai-inspired fish curry made with sour and flaky fish, blended with a rich and creamy curry sauce.

ingredients.name: Sour Fish Fillets
ingredients.notes: Flaky and slightly sour fish fillets, cut into 1-inch pieces

ingredients.name: Thai Red Curry Paste
ingredients.notes: Made with a blend of spices, lemongrass, and galangal

ingredients.name: Coconut Milk
ingredients.notes: Full-fat coconut milk for a rich and creamy sauce

ingredients.name: Fish Sauce
ingredients.notes: Salty and umami-rich fish sauce, made from fermented fish and salt

ingredients.name: Brown Sugar
ingredients.notes: Adds a touch of sweetness to balance the flavors

ingredients.name: Red Bell Pepper
ingredients.notes: Sliced red bell pepper, adds a pop of color and sweetness

instructions: Heat oil in a large pan over medium-high heat.
Add the Thai red curry paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Add the sour fish fillets and cook for 2-3 minutes, until they start to flake apart.
Pour in the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Add the fish sauce, brown sugar, and Thai basil leaves. Stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and let the curry simmer for 5-7 minutes, until the sauce has thickened and the fish is cooked through.
Stir in the sliced red bell pepper and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the pepper is tender.
Season the curry with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the Thai fish curry hot, garnished with additional Thai basil leaves if desired.
